AI_Description: Does the sentence use a number to count people or objects (e.g. 'three cats' or '500 people')?
Does the sentence state someone's age (e.g. 'is 45 years old')?
Does the sentence ask for or state a price/cost (e.g. 'costs 45 Euros' or 'is 7 pounds')?
Does the sentence ask what time it is (e.g. 'What's the time' or 'Do you have the time')?
Does the sentence state a specific time (e.g. 'at three o'clock' or 'It is 9.45')?
Does the sentence use 'there is' or 'there are' to state something exists (e.g. 'There is a bank' or 'There are a lot of seats')?
Does the sentence describe a habit or routine using a simple present tense verb (e.g. 'She eats fruit every day' or 'We go to the beach')?
Does the sentence describe something happening right now using 'is/are' + verb-ing (e.g. 'He is studying' or 'It is raining')?
Does the sentence use 'is', 'am', or 'are' to describe or identify someone or something (e.g. 'I am a student' or 'France is wonderful')?
Does the sentence use 'have got' or 'has got' to express possession (e.g. 'I have got all his CDs' or 'Have you got any money')?
Does the sentence give a direct instruction or command (e.g. 'Sit down please' or 'Don't talk to the driver')?
Does the sentence ask a question using a question word or auxiliary verb (e.g. 'What is your name?' or 'Do you like dancing?')?
Does the sentence join two clauses using 'and', 'but', or 'because' (e.g. 'She lives in Switzerland and she goes skiing')?
Does the sentence use an adverb of frequency like 'always', 'sometimes', or 'never' (e.g. 'We always go shopping on Saturdays')?
Does the sentence compare two or more things using '-er' or '-est' (e.g. 'She is taller than Michelle' or 'Tom is the oldest')?
Example_Pattern: Subject + is + Adjective-er + than / Subject + is + the + Adjective-est

AI_Description: Does the sentence use a preposition of place or time like 'in', 'on', or 'at' (e.g. 'He is sitting at the table' or 'The holidays begin in July')?
Does the sentence state personal information like name, origin, or occupation (e.g. 'My name is Carlos' or 'I live in Beirut')?
Does the sentence state a specific time using clock expressions (e.g. 'It is nine forty-five' or 'The train leaves at three o'clock')?
Example_Pattern: It + is + Time / at + Time Expression

AI_Description: Does the sentence give or ask for directions (e.g. 'Turn right' or 'The hotel is on the left')?
Example_Pattern: Verb + Direction / Noun + is + on the + left/right

AI_Description: Does the sentence use a possessive adjective or 's to show ownership (e.g. 'This is my seat' or 'It is Mary's turn')?
